questions.question: What slice of multi-agent engineering does each of the three canonical 2026 case studies illustrate?
options: Three vendors selling the same architecture
Anthropic Research: supervisor-worker at production scale; MetaGPT/ChatDev: SOP-encoded role decomposition; OpenClaw/Moltbook: population-scale emergence with millions of agents
Three sizes of the same chatbot
Cloud, on-prem, and edge deployments
questions.explanation: Reading them as a set beats reading them alone: each covers a different region of the design space, and together they pin the common patterns the capstone distills.

questions.question: What are the four design lessons codified from Anthropic's Research system?
options: Bigger models, more tokens, more agents, more rounds
Scale effort to query complexity, broad-then-narrow search, rainbow deployments for stateful agents, and verification is not optional — the system hallucinated without explicit verifier roles
Cache everything, retry everything, log everything, bill everything
One model, one prompt, one tool, one answer
questions.explanation: Backed by the published numbers: +90.2% over single-agent Opus 4, 80% of BrowseComp variance from token usage alone, at 15x tokens per query — multi-agent pays when the task justifies it.

questions.question: How does MacNet scale ChatDev-style role decomposition past 1,000 agents?
options: By using a single giant group chat
DAGs: each node is a role specialization, edges encode handoff contracts — scale is possible because routing is explicit and offline-computable, not negotiated in chat
By giving every agent the same prompt
By removing all verification
questions.explanation: The structural lesson generalizes: structure beats size (a tight 5-role SOP team beats 50 unstructured agents), written handoff schemas beat free-form chat, and when the flow is knowable you should encode it.

questions.question: What did the Moltbook episode demonstrate about population-scale agent substrates?
options: Nothing notable happened

~2.3M agent accounts within days, emergent economic activity, prompt-injection propagating through thousands of agent-to-agent interactions in hours, and state-level regulation within weeks (China's OpenClaw restriction)
Population scale eliminates security risks
Agent social networks are commercially unviable
questions.explanation: Millions of agents on a shared substrate produced both emergent markets and viral attack surfaces — and drew a Meta acquisition plus government restriction in the same month. Scale changes the threat model qualitatively.

questions.question: What is the April 2026 framework landscape in one pass?
options: One framework won; all others died

LangGraph and CrewAI lead production; AG2 continues community AutoGen while Microsoft's merged into Agent Framework; OpenAI Agents SDK succeeds Swarm; Google ADK is the A2A-native entrant — and every major framework ships MCP support
All frameworks were deprecated in favor of raw API calls
Only proprietary frameworks remain
questions.explanation: Protocol convergence is the quiet headline: MCP everywhere, A2A in most. Framework choice now reduces to the four-primitive axes from earlier in the phase, not protocol lock-in.
